welcome:
Welcome to Annotix! 🎯

A professional tool for annotating images and time series for machine learning model training. Supports multiple annotation types: Bounding Boxes, OBB, Segmentation, Keypoints, Polygons, Landmarks, Time Series and Classification.
installation:
📦 Local installation

Annotix installs completely on your PC as a Progressive Web App (PWA). It doesn't require internet connection after initial installation. Works offline and runs directly from your browser.
dataStorage:
💾 Data storage

IMPORTANT: All your data is saved in the browser's IndexedDB, not on a server. This means:
• ✅ Total privacy: your data never leaves your PC
• ✅ Works without internet
• ⚠️ Risk: if you clear browser data, everything will be lost
projectBackup:
💼 Project backup

To avoid data loss:
• Export your projects in .tix format (contains everything: images, annotations, classes)
• You can reload the .tix file at any time
• Share .tix projects with other users
• Also export .tixconfig files to share only class configuration
createProjectButton: Click New Project to begin. The creation modal will open for you to choose the project type.
projectTypeSelection:
📋 Project types

In the modal you can choose from 8 different types:
• Bounding Box: Rectangular boxes (object detection)
• OBB: Oriented rotated boxes
• Mask: Pixel segmentation
• Keypoints: Key points (pose estimation)
• Polygon: Free-form polygons
• Landmarks: Facial points
• Time Series: Temporal series
• Classification: Image classification
projectName:
✏️ Project name

In the modal, enter a descriptive name for your project. For example: 'Car Detection 2024' or 'Cell Segmentation'.

The project type will determine which tools are available in the canvas.
classesPanel: This is the class management panel. Here you define the categories you'll annotate.
classesInWorkflow:
🏷️ Class management

Classes can be added in two ways:

1. In the project creation modal: Define your main classes from the start

2. In the right panel (current): Add, edit or delete classes at any time during work

Each class has a unique name, a distinctive color and a sequential ID. Use number keys 1-9 to quickly select classes.
gallery: The image gallery displays all images in your project. You can filter by all, annotated or unannotated. Thumbnails show the status of each image.
loadImages: Press Load Images to import images to your project. Supports multiple formats: JPG, PNG, WEBP. You can also use Data Augmentation to automatically generate variations of your images.
workspace: The main workspace where you visualize and annotate your images or time series. Use the mouse to draw, wheel to zoom, and hold middle button or use Pan tool to move the view.
tools: The floating tools change according to project type. Include: Box (B), OBB (O), Mask (M), Select (V) and Pan (H). Each tool has specific controls like brush size for masks or zoom controls.
stats: The statistics panel shows your project progress: total images, annotated images, total labels and number of classes. The progress bar helps you visualize how much work you've completed.
exportButton: The Export button allows you to download your dataset. Let's see the available options...
exportFormats:
📦 Export formats

In the export modal you'll find multiple options:

• YOLO Detection: .txt with normalized bounding boxes
• YOLO Segmentation: .txt with mask polygons
• YOLO OBB: .txt with oriented boxes
• COCO JSON: Standard COCO format
• U-Net Masks: PNG images for segmentation
• Pascal VOC XML: Pascal VOC format
• CSV: Data table
trainingCode:
🐍 Training code

In the export modal, you'll find the option 'Generate Python training code'.

This creates a complete example script to train your model with YOLOv8 using Ultralytics:
• Automatic path configuration
• Training script with optimal hyperparameters
• Ready to run: just install dependencies and train
shortcuts: Press the keyboard button or use F1 to see all available keyboard shortcuts. Include Ctrl+S to save, Ctrl+Z to undo, number keys to select classes, and more.
finish:
Ready to start! 🚀

Remember:
• Save frequently with Ctrl+S
• Export backups in .tix format
• Your data is only in your browser

If you need help, press the ? button to see this tour again.
